comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Claimed from merged origin/main dabf4c6ce2afe84ef8b4634f361d2002c673e9bd after review-plan --status confirmed a certified attestation. Beginning held-out TDD implementation.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: RED oracle confirmed before production edits. Test contract: authoritative source is the ticket AC plus AnalyzerResult.loc and typed [code_health] config contracts. Tier: pure function unit tests for size arithmetic, registry contract tests for analyzer adaptation/provenance, and real metrics command interface tests for config context and missing-tool fallback. Preconditions are exact synthetic LOC payloads/config or a foreign Go repo with PATH emptied. Postconditions are exact counts/bounds, one structural spec per id, cap-change deregistration, preserved trend Unavailable, and no fabricated zeros/raw errno. RED run: 10 required failures for the intended missing behavior (old signature/hardcodes, absent analyzer seam/context fields, git provenance, cap-change registration, leaked FileNotFoundError); 5 pre-existing churn/default-window contracts stayed green.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Held-out validation and local preflight complete. Standard-tier implementer: 1 substantive attempt. Full restored oracle: 15 passed. Defect-seeded mutation changed over-cap from > to >=; held-out exact-cap boundary failed (2 vs 1) and returned green after restoration. Refactoring litmus passes: tests assert public function values, registry contracts, and real command output; provenance/uniqueness assertions are explicit public contracts. Gates: make lint green; mypy green over 379 source files; make test green with 7,091 passed, 37 skipped, 114 deselected. Five-axis review found no blockers: exact boundary/no-cap behavior, config-backed SCC adaptation, structural provenance, honest Unavailable, deleted legacy cap reader, no dependency/security expansion, and files 198/150/163 LOC. SCC is invoked once per size spec by this ticket's prescribed adapter; the already-recorded dependent story c23 owns per-evaluation composition/caching.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Pushed Gerrit 948 (Change-Id I9721d378d704ca485aa569e6313dd4163889904e). Gerrit 947 was immediately abandoned as an identical superseded review created while correcting literal newline formatting in the commit message; no code changed between them.

compiled_state.description:
## Problem
Migrate the code_health size metrics off the hardcoded `src/rebar` scan + `.github/module-size-limit.txt` onto the scc LOC analyzer + `[code_health]` config, so a non-Python client gets honest Unavailable instead of fabricated zeros. Does NOT touch the CI module-size GATE (separate governance).

## Mechanism (G6 — grounded in the existing registry adapter; positive rationale)
The registry wires metrics via a single-arg context adapter `_spec(id, fn, ranged)` whose `compute(ctx)` reads `ctx.repo_root` (+since/until) and calls `fn(...)`. This ticket adds a parallel ANALYZER adapter and reworks the size fns:
1. `_analyzer_spec(id, fn)`: its `compute(ctx)` obtains the aggregate LOC by calling the scc LOC analyzer `scc_loc.analyze(ctx.repo_root, ctx.scan_roots)` `[rebar:18f9-5b75-b909-408c]` (module size is POLYGLOT aggregate LOC, so it uses the single language-agnostic LOC analyzer directly — NOT `resolve_analyzer(lang)`, which is for per-language complexity/duplication). If `analyze` returns `Unavailable`, `compute` returns `None` (-> the registry renders Unavailable). Otherwise it calls `fn(analyzer_result.loc, ctx.size_cap, ctx.size_near_fraction)` — a UNIFORM 3-arg call; every registered fn takes exactly `(loc, size_cap, size_near_fraction)` (oversized_module_count ignores the last). Rationale: mirrors `_spec`, keeps analyzer invocation in one adapter; using the LOC analyzer directly (vs per-language resolve) matches the polyglot aggregate nature of size metrics.
2. Config onto ctx: `_commands/metrics.py` (today builds `ctx = SimpleNamespace(repo_root, since, until)`) is extended to also set `scan_roots`, `size_cap`, `size_near_fraction` from the `[code_health]` config `[rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb]`.
3. Fns over the loc payload (`loc = {"files": {path:int}, "max_loc": int}`, shape from `[rebar:18f9-5b75-b909-408c]`):
   - `module_size_distribution(loc, size_cap, size_near_fraction) -> {"count","near_cap_count","over_cap_count","max_loc"}`: count=len(files); max_loc=loc["max_loc"]. If `size_cap is None`: over_cap_count and near_cap_count are `None` (no cap configured -> not computable); else over_cap_count=#files with loc>size_cap; near_cap_count=#files with `size_cap*(1-size_near_fraction) <= loc <= size_cap` (BOTH bounds inclusive; the near band is at-or-below the cap and excludes over-cap files).
   - `oversized_module_count(loc, size_cap, size_near_fraction) -> int | None` (UNIFORM 3-arg signature to match the adapter call in §1; `size_near_fraction` is accepted but unused): #files>size_cap, or `None` when `size_cap is None` (-> Unavailable).
4. Remove `_LIMIT_FILE`/`_read_cap`/the `src/rebar` rglob from git_metrics. DEREGISTER `cap_change_events` (remove from `register()`). REMOVE the `oversized_module_count` SEED MetricSpec (registry.py:142-149, `compute=_no_data_yet`) so git_metrics' real spec is the only one; `module_size_trend` seed (registry.py:134-141) STAYS as a stub returning **Unavailable** (a scc-based snapshot WRITER to feed a trend series does not exist — OUT OF SCOPE / follow-up); `commit_cadence_trend` seed is untouched.
5. PROVENANCE (T8): the analyzer-derived `module_size_distribution` / `oversized_module_count` MetricSpec entries carry `source="structural"`, `lens="code_health"`, and `accruing_since="2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00"` (the value the existing metrics reader modules use).

## Grounding (E4 — verified via Read)
- CONFIRMED git_metrics.py `_LIMIT_FILE`:35, `_read_cap`:75, `src/rebar` rglob :93, `cap_change_events`:167 registered ~:272; `_spec`/`register` in the registry-integration region (compute(ctx) reads ctx.repo_root/since/until).
- CONFIRMED registry.py: `module_size_trend` seed :134-141, `oversized_module_count` seed :142-149 (`compute=_no_data_yet`) — the latter is REMOVED here.
- CONFIRMED `_commands/metrics.py` builds `ctx = SimpleNamespace(repo_root, since, until)` (extended here).
- Contracts: scc `scc_loc.analyze` + loc payload `[rebar:18f9-5b75-b909-408c]`; `AnalyzerResult`/`Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` `[rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]`; config keys `size_cap:int|None`/`size_near_fraction:float`/`scan_roots:list[str]` `[rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb]` (all depends_on edges recorded).
- OLD-behavior tests to update: `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py`, `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics_heldout.py`.

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] No metric reader references the old hardcodes. PROVE: `! grep -Eq "src/rebar|module-size-limit.txt" src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`.
- [ ] Positive path: synthetic `loc={"files":{"a":100,"b":850},"max_loc":850}`, size_cap=800, size_near_fraction=0.1 (near band 720..800) -> module_size_distribution count==2, over_cap_count==1 (b=850>800), near_cap_count==0, max_loc==850; oversized_module_count==1. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_configured_positive`.
- [ ] near-cap boundary: with size_cap=800, size_near_fraction=0.1 (band 720..800 inclusive), files {a:720 (lower edge, near), b:800 (upper edge=cap, near), c:719 (below, not near), d:801 (over, not near)} -> near_cap_count==2, over_cap_count==1. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_near_cap_boundary`.
- [ ] size_cap=None -> over_cap_count/near_cap_count None; oversized_module_count returns None (Unavailable). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_no_cap`.
- [ ] analyzer-derived specs carry `source="structural"`; the `oversized_module_count` seed stub is removed (only one spec of that id). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_provenance_and_no_dup_seed`.
- [ ] `cap_change_events` deregistered; `module_size_trend` returns Unavailable. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py::test_cap_change_events_deregistered tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_trend_unavailable`.
- [ ] Unconfigured analyzer (scc_loc.analyze -> Unavailable) -> Unavailable, NOT `{count:0,max_loc:0}` authoritative, no leaked errno. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py::test_foreign_repo_honest_unavailable`.
- [ ] churn / refactor_to_addition_ratio + agent_process unchanged; updated git_metrics tests green. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics_heldout.py`.

## Dependencies: 3b30, 18f9, 21fb, 3ba0, e7a0 (recorded depends_on).
## Files likely touched
- src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py (add `_analyzer_spec` + reworked fns; remove hardcodes + `cap_change_events`)
- src/rebar/metrics/registry.py (remove the `oversized_module_count` seed stub)
- src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py (ctx carries the [code_health] config)
- tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py, tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics_heldout.py, tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py
## Estimated scope: M-L

file_impact.reason: read via analyzer; drop src/rebar + limit-file; deregister cap_change_events
wire module_size_trend to snapshot store; remove cap_change_events spec
regression: honest Unavailable, no fabricated zeros

compiled_state.title: Migrate code_health readers onto the analyzer; drop hardcoded src/rebar + module-size cap file
